title: Twilio API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Twilio Account SID detected. Account SIDs uniquely identify your Twilio account and are used alongside Auth Tokens for API authentication. Exposing Account SIDs combined with Auth Tokens provides complete access to your Twilio resources including phone numbers, messaging, voice calls, and billing information. Store Account SIDs securely and rotate Auth Tokens immediately if exposure is suspected.

Twilio API Key SID detected. API Key SIDs are used with API Key Secrets to authenticate Twilio API requests as an alternative to Account SID/Auth Token pairs. Exposing API Key SIDs provides access to Twilio services including messaging, voice, video, and account management based on configured permissions. Rotate API Key Secrets immediately and review API key permissions.

Twilio Auth Token detected in variable assignment. Auth Tokens are secret keys used with Account SIDs to authenticate API requests to Twilio services. Exposing Auth Tokens provides full access to messaging, voice calls, phone number management, and billing operations. Rotate this token immediately in your Twilio Console and use secure credential storage.

Twilio API Key Secret detected in variable assignment. API Key Secrets are used with API Key SIDs to authenticate Twilio API requests. These secrets provide programmatic access to Twilio services including messaging, voice, video, and account operations based on configured permissions. Rotate this API Key Secret immediately and ensure secure credential storage.
